recent_calls.summary: The user requested to be connected to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to schedule an appointment for a cooling issue under a home warranty. The agent gathered information about the problem and confirmed it was not an emergency. The user did not provide a dispatch number but expressed openness to scheduling.
The user requested to be connected to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.

The user called to set up a service appointment for a cooling issue under their home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that the request would be passed to the team for follow-up. The user confirmed the summary but indicated a change was needed before the call ended.

The user called to set an appointment for their home warranty. The agent informed the user that such appointments are handled differently and that the request would be passed to the appropriate team for follow-up. The call ended after this exchange.

The user expressed dislike for AI during the call, and the agent responded by transferring the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another number.

Timothy Smith called to schedule a cooling service appointment under his home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and offered available time slots on April 21st, which Timothy accepted for 12 PM. The call ended with the appointment successfully booked.

Ashley from American Wholesale called to inquire about heating service in Silex for an AC unit issue. The agent confirmed service availability, collected detailed information, and offered appointment slots, but the user declined the proposed times and requested alternative dates. The call ended with the agent noting the user's preferred dates for follow-up.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for an AC unit that isn't turning on under an American Home Shield home warranty but did not have the dispatch number. The agent took the details and assured the user that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment. The user rated the experience positively but expressed a dislike for AI.

Karen called to report that her air conditioning system is running but not cooling, with the thermostat set to 60 degrees while the house remains at 78 degrees. She requested service around 5 PM the same day, and the agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her soon. Karen expressed interest in receiving information about free maintenance programs if available.
The call was a brief interaction where the agent greeted the user and attempted to assist, but the user only confirmed if the agent could hear them before the call ended abruptly.

The user called to follow up on a case involving a warranty claim related to a service visit by Devon. The agent attempted to assist but the user requested a live agent, leading to the call being transferred to another representative.

The user called to request an electrician to come out, specifying it was not an emergency but they needed to speak with a live person. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Tyanna Elam called to schedule a service appointment for her central air unit, which is not working. The agent collected her contact details and confirmed the soonest available appointment. Tyanna expressed interest in home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the communication during the call.

Angela from American Home Shield called Budget to clarify a work order related to air conditioning issues. She requested a live agent to discuss the possibility of a return visit for the member, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user called to inquire about the delay and cost of replacement caps and Freon for their central air conditioning, expressing confusion about charges despite coverage by American Home Shield. The agent acknowledged the concerns and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The call ended with the user being transferred for more specialized help.

Joe Galvin called to clarify how to send payment upfront for a furnace replacement under his American Home Shield home warranty. He expressed difficulty with computers and was transferred to the billing team for assistance. The agent confirmed the service details and arranged the transfer to resolve the payment issue.

The user, Eve from American Home Shield, called to follow up on a dispatch regarding the installation of two coils for an air conditioning system. The agent confirmed the dispatch details, updated the contact phone number, and assured that the team would follow up as soon as possible. The call ended with the user satisfied with the updated information.

The user called to inquire about appliance repair services, specifically for a washer with a broken belt. The agent informed the user that they do not service household appliances but focus on heating, cooling, plumbing, and indoor air quality systems. The user declined further assistance and ended the call.

The user called to schedule a service for a cooling system issue likely caused by a bad capacitor on the blower fan, through American Home Shield. The agent collected all necessary details including the service address and user information, and confirmed the summary with the user. The user agreed to receive information about maintenance programs and declined to provide feedback.

Carlos called to inquire about a missed air conditioning service appointment at his house and requested to be transferred to Jason, the contractor handling the job. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to Jason. No emergency was reported, and the user had already spoken to someone previously.

The user called Budget regarding a previous heating and cooling service, expressing concern that something was missing after the service. The user indicated the issue was urgent and requested to speak directly with a service technician. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for immediate assistance.

Sandra called to schedule a standard air conditioner tune-up under her whole house plan. The agent booked her for Wednesday, April 22nd at 8 AM and confirmed the details. Sandra provided feedback rating the AI assistant a 4 and requested to speak with a human, after which the call was transferred to a live agent.

Andrea called to speak with the technician about the estimate for her air conditioning. The agent acknowledged the issue and confirmed that the technician would call Andrea back to discuss the estimate. The call ended with the agent assuring a follow-up call would be made.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for an air conditioning issue where the exterior unit is cavitating. The agent collected the dispatch number, service address, contact information, and confirmed the details with the user. The user declined to provide feedback, and the agent informed them that a human agent would follow up to confirm the appointment.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate representative. The call ended shortly after the transfer was successful.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.

Stan from American Home Shield called to inquire about dispatch services for an air conditioner on the roof. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

Jacques called to confirm if a follow-up home warranty service visit was still scheduled for 4 PM today to check the air pressure on his air conditioning unit. The agent confirmed the details, noted the potential weather impact, and informed Jacques that the team would reach out to confirm the appointment. Jacques declined information about maintenance programs but provided positive feedback about the call.

The user called back to inform the agent that they found another technician and did not need to make an appointment with Budget. The agent acknowledged the message and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Robert called to report an issue with his thermostat buttons sticking and was told by a technician that replacement would be out of pocket, but his home warranty company indicated it should be covered. The agent assured Robert that the team would review the situation and follow up with him. The call ended with the agent confirming contact details and promising a follow-up.
The user requested to be connected to a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user called to check the status of an HVAC tune-up appointment scheduled through their home warranty. The agent confirmed the user's details and dispatch number, and informed them that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Joe Galvin called to confirm he accepted a noncover cost estimate for furnace repair and requested a follow-up call to schedule the service once the team is ready. The agent confirmed the details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Joe declined. Joe provided positive feedback, rating the experience a five and appreciating the agent's knowledge.

Pamela called to report a leak on her furnace and requested a callback as soon as possible. The agent confirmed the details and assured her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. The agent also mentioned home maintenance programs but the call ended before Pamela responded.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly shortly after the agent greeted the user and began to speak. There was no meaningful interaction or task completed during the call.

The user called to follow up on a service call regarding their AC unit freezing after being off for two days. The agent booked an appointment for April 20th at 8 AM and confirmed the details with the user. The user was satisfied with the communication and gave positive feedback.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called Budget and requested to speak with someone. The agent, Sophie, initiated a transfer to a live agent to assist the user. The call was successfully transferred after a brief interaction.

Anna from American Home Shield called to report a cooling system issue at 5749 Floyd Avenue, Saint Louis, MO. The agent confirmed the details including the dispatch number and phone number, and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested. The call was completed successfully with all necessary information gathered.

The user called to clarify a misunderstanding regarding payment for a thermostat replacement and mentioned having a technician working on their heating and cooling system. The agent acknowledged the billing-related issue and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.

The user called to request a thermostat replacement covered by their home warranty, expressing frustration about the thermostat's malfunctioning temperature buttons. The agent confirmed the service needed and attempted to schedule an appointment, but the user insisted on having the replacement done immediately while the technician was already at the house. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Kristen, called to check on the status of her technician appointment scheduled between 8:15 and 9:15 AM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance as the technician had not yet arrived.

The user, Julia from Wansby, called on behalf of Luby Equipment to inquire about an employee named Daniel LaGrace and requested to speak with someone directly. The agent informed her that they couldn't confirm employment details but would pass the request to the team and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to follow up on a work order related to air conditioning at a property, but the call ended abruptly before the agent could gather more details or schedule a service appointment.

The user called to inquire about a work order from American Home Shield for a central air unit issue where the outside unit runs but no air blows inside. The agent gathered the user's information and explained that someone from Budget would contact her soon to confirm service. The user declined information about maintenance programs and expressed urgency due to the heat.

Floyd called to reschedule a technician visit for a bathroom ceiling fan service at his home in Hillsboro, Missouri. The agent confirmed details and noted Floyd's preference for the appointment to be scheduled for tomorrow. The call ended after confirming Floyd's contact number and availability.
The user requested to speak to a customer service agent regarding a work order. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

The user called to book a furnace cleaning service at 4630 Wright Avenue, St. Louis, MO, and preferred a Friday morning appointment. The agent provided information about the service fee and confirmed the user's details. The booking was noted, but a human agent will follow up to confirm availability and answer further questions.
